Biography
Biography
Kevin Amorin is a part-time lecturer at the Khoury College of Computer Sciences at Northeastern University. Areas he teaches include information assurance, system and networks, and network security practices.
Currently, Amorin is a software engineering manager at Google. He has more than 15 years of industry experience, including at organizations such as Microsoft, Motorola, and BitSight, where he was an engineering director responsible for delivering their cyber risk rating platform. Amorin also worked for seven years as the network security manager at Harvard Kennedy School. Since 2008, Amorin has been the coach and advisor to the Northeastern Cyber Defense team. The team competes in the Collegiate Cyber Defense Competition, an annual three-day event designed to help students sharpen their skills and boost their knowledge for administering and protecting a large corporate network. Northeastern’s team won the Northeast regional competition three times and won the 2010 national title against over 80 universities.
